pages.body: SIP Trunking — VoiceTel Carrier-grade SIP trunks for PBX, UC, contact center, SBC, Asterisk, FreePBX, FreeSWITCH, and FusionPBX deployments. SIP Trunking Carrier SIP trunks for systems you already run. Connect PBX, UC, contact-center, and SBC environments to VoiceTel for PSTN origination, termination, numbers, emergency-service support, and route controls without replacing your call platform. h1: Wholesale voice for applications, carriers, and operators.

pages.body: Voice — VoiceTel Wholesale programmable voice with origination, termination, VoiceML call control, STIR/SHAKEN, recordings, conferences, queues, and signed webhooks. Programmable voice Wholesale voice for applications, carriers, and operators. Build voice workflows on VoiceTel without paying retail markup on every layer of the call. Use API-driven calling, SIP access, recordings, conferences, streams, transcriptions, queues, and status callbacks on a platform built for telecom teams. See pricing Explore VoiceML Voice capabilities Origination and termination Run inbound and outbound voice through VoiceTel with published rate keys, routing controls, and carrier-support escalation paths. VoiceML call control Answer, route, record, gather, dial, queue, conference, redirect, stream, and transcribe calls using XML-based voice markup. Signed webhooks Status, recording, gather, dial, conference, AMD, and action callbacks use signed webhook delivery for request verification. Bring your own media vendors Use your preferred speech-to-text, text-to-speech, and recording storage providers where supported; VoiceTel does not hide third-party provider costs inside vague platform claims. Operational proof points STIR/SHAKEN aware Caller identity and attestation are part of the voice story, not an afterthought. Six US POPs NYC, CLT, ORD, DEN, DFW, and SJC are used throughout the site as the published network locations. Operator support Escalations reach people who can read SIP traces, carrier responses, and webhook logs. Percent-savings claims appear only when approved VoiceTel public rates support the line-item math. Voice FAQ Can existing Twilio voice applications migrate? Yes, when they stay within the supported VoiceML-compatible voice surface. Review the compatibility matrix before production cutover. Does VoiceTel provide speech services directly? Speech services are positioned as configurable or bring-your-own unless a specific managed VoiceTel bundle is confirmed. How are rates displayed? Rates are source-dated and shown with unit, source, and assumptions.

Compatibility Know exactly which call features are supported before migration. The VoiceML compatibility page is explicit, searchable, and honest. Every verb, attribute, REST resource, and caveat is published as a clear support status rather than a broad promise. Paste XML Migration guide Matrix summary Supported Core call-control verbs and voice resources that engineering has verified. Native VoiceTel path Products such as numbers and messaging link to VoiceTel-native product pages when they are not part of the compatible voice API surface. h1: Move compatible programmable voice workloads without rewriting call logic.

pages.body: VoiceML — VoiceTel VoiceML is VoiceTel's XML-based programmable voice runtime for compatible call control, signed webhooks, recordings, conferences, queues, and SDK migration paths. Voice markup runtime Move compatible programmable voice workloads without rewriting call logic. VoiceML is VoiceTel's XML-based voice runtime for teams that already have voice markup, callbacks, recordings, conferences, queues, and API-driven call flows. Keep the interaction model; move the economics and support path to VoiceTel. Start migration Compatibility matrix VoiceML example The markup stays readable, inspectable, and easy to test before cutover. <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <Response> <Say>Welcome. Please hold while we connect your call.</Say> <Dial timeout="20" callerId="+12124016220"> <Number>+15555551234</Number> </Dial> </Response> Compatible voice surface Calls Create, fetch, update, list, and end calls using the supported voice-call lifecycle surface. Recordings Start, pause, resume, stop, list, retrieve, and delete supported recordings. Conferences and queues Use conference resources, participants, recordings, queue CRUD, and queue members where supported. Streams, transcriptions, and SIP recording Start and inspect supported live media, transcription, and SIP recording resources with documented caveats. Applications Save voice-only application defaults and resolve them from call creation where supported. Webhook verification Use signed form-encoded callbacks so existing webhook validation patterns remain familiar. What's different Voice-focused compatibility The compatible SDK surface is voice-focused. Messaging, number provisioning, pricing APIs, and other unrelated CPaaS products use native VoiceTel product APIs or portal workflows. Unsupported payment verb The payment verb is intentionally not implemented because PCI scope belongs in a fully certified payment flow. Number provisioning path DIDs are managed through VoiceTel number workflows, not through the compatible customer-facing voice API surface. Start with one production call flow. Pick a known call path, paste the markup into the compatibility checker, run a test call, then compare the bill line by line. Migrate from Twilio Open calculator

pages.title: Migrate from Twilio — VoiceTel
pages.description: A practical migration page for compatible Twilio voice applications moving to VoiceTel VoiceML with signed webhooks, SDK host overrides, and line-item pricing math.

h1: Move compatible voice workflows in measured steps.
h2: What stays the same; what is checked

pages.body: Migrate from Twilio — VoiceTel A practical migration page for compatible Twilio voice applications moving to VoiceTel VoiceML with signed webhooks, SDK host overrides, and line-item pricing math. Migration Move compatible voice workflows in measured steps. Keep the call-control model your application already uses, point supported voice requests at VoiceTel, verify signed webhooks, and cut over only after behavior and cost are proven. Check compatibility Run calculator Four-step migration 1. Inventory your call flows List inbound URLs, outbound call creation paths, recordings, conferences, queues, streams, transcriptions, AMD, and callback URLs. 2. Check markup compatibility Paste representative XML into the compatibility checker and review supported, partial, and unsupported verbs before live traffic. 3. Change the API destination Use an SDK HTTP-client override or a direct HTTP call to send compatible voice API requests to VoiceTel. 4. Verify callbacks and cut over Confirm signatures, status events, recording callbacks, retry behavior, and billing assumptions before moving production numbers. Direct HTTP test call A one-call test proves credentials, routing, webhook reachability, and basic call control before deeper migration work. curl -u "$VOICETEL_ACCOUNT_SID:$VOICETEL_API_KEY" \ -d "To=+18005551234" \ -d "From=+18005550000" \ -d "Url=https://example.com/voice.xml" \ "https://api.voicetel.com/2010-04-01/Accounts/$VOICETEL_ACCOUNT_SID/Calls.json" What stays the same; what is checked Same model XML voice markup, form-encoded webhooks, request signatures, account-style credentials, and voice-call lifecycle resources stay familiar. Checked before launch Any payment, non-voice, phone-number provisioning, or messaging API calls is identified and routed to native VoiceTel products or removed from the compatible voice path. Measured economics Compare a real monthly workload, not a broad slogan. Migration pricing comparison Show a source-dated comparison for the exact workload: inbound minutes, outbound minutes, AMD calls, recordings, media features, numbers, and messages if the migration includes messaging. Percentage claims appear only when backed by a line-item table. Bring one call flow and one bill. VoiceTel can validate compatibility and model the current workload before you move production traffic. Contact VoiceTel
